methods.method_name: Analytical Procedures and Exposure Age Calculations
methods.description: Process for preparing samples for 10 Be and 14 C measurement and conducting analyses to determine exposure ages.

steps.description: Samples were prepared for 10 Be measurement at Lamont-Doherty Earth Observatory cosmogenic nuclide laboratory (LDEO), and for in situ 14 C measurement at both LDEO (1 sample) and Tulane University Cosmogenic Nuclide Laboratory (8 samples).
steps.searchable_summary: prepare samples for 10 Be and 14 C measurement

steps.description: 10 Be analyses were undertaken at the Center for Accelerator Mass Spectrometry, Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, USA (CAMS), and Scottish Universities Environmental Research Centre, UK.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct 10 Be analyses at CAMS and SUERC

steps.description: 14 C was measured at CAMS and Woods Hole National Ocean Sciences Accelerator Mass Spectrometry facility, USA.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct 14 C measurements at CAMS and Woods Hole

steps.description: Details of all analytical procedures, exposure age calculations and analytical data are provided in Tables S3 and S4, and 10 Be data are publicly accessible at the UK Polar Data Centre.
steps.searchable_summary: access analytical data and exposure age calculations

methods.description: Strategy for identifying and collecting rock samples for surface exposure dating.

steps.description: Satellite imagery was used prior to the field campaign to identify sites likely to support erratic cobbles and boulders based on slope angle and presence of visible drift.
steps.searchable_summary: use satellite imagery to identify sampling sites

steps.description: A total of 148 rock samples were collected from several nunataks and ridges surrounding Mt Murphy.
steps.searchable_summary: collect rock samples from identified sites

steps.description: Position and elevation measurements for each sample were made using a Trimble GPS 5700 receiver.
steps.searchable_summary: measure position and elevation of samples using GPS

steps.description: The GNSS observations were post-processed using Precise Point Positioning in Bernese processing software.
steps.searchable_summary: post-process GNSS observations using Bernese software

steps.description: The elevation of the modern ice surface was derived from a Digital Elevation Model (DEM).
steps.searchable_summary: derive ice surface elevation from DEM
